GitHub

Audio Player

Native HTML5 <audio> element with built-in controls

Live Examples

Basic Audio Player

Autoplay Audio

Looping Audio

Multiple Audio Formats

Browser APIs

Native <audio> Element

Built-in to all modern browsers with native player controls.

No Custom JavaScript Required

Full playback functionality works automatically with browser UI.

Keyboard Support

Space (play/pause), arrow keys work natively for seeking.

Multiple Format Support

Specify fallback formats with <source> elements.

Preload Control

Optimize loading with preload="none", "metadata", or "auto".

Fully Accessible

Screen readers announce controls, keyboard navigable.

HTML Structure

Basic Audio with Controls

<audio controls>
  <source src="audio.mp3" type="audio/mpeg">
  Your browser does not support the audio element.
</audio>

Audio with Multiple Formats

<audio controls>
  <source src="audio.mp3" type="audio/mpeg">
  <source src="audio.ogg" type="audio/ogg">
  <source src="audio.wav" type="audio/wav">
  Your browser does not support the audio element.
</audio>

Audio Attributes

<audio 
  controls            <!-- Show player controls -->
  autoplay            <!-- Start playing automatically -->
  loop                <!-- Loop when finished -->
  muted               <!-- Mute by default -->
  preload="metadata"  <!-- none, metadata, auto -->
>
  <source src="audio.mp3" type="audio/mpeg">
</audio>

Audio with Fallback Content

<audio controls>
  <source src="audio.mp3" type="audio/mpeg">
  <p>
    Your browser doesn't support HTML5 audio. 
    <a href="audio.mp3">Download the file</a> instead.
  </p>
</audio>

JavaScript API

Basic Controls

const audio = document.querySelector('audio');

// Play/pause
audio.play();
audio.pause();

// Get current time and duration
console.log(audio.currentTime);  // seconds
console.log(audio.duration);     // seconds

// Set playback position
audio.currentTime = 30;

// Volume control (0 to 1)
audio.volume = 0.5;
audio.muted = true;

// Playback rate
audio.playbackRate = 1.5; // 1.5x speed

Event Handling

const audio = document.querySelector('audio');

// Listen for events
audio.addEventListener('play', () => console.log('Playing'));
audio.addEventListener('pause', () => console.log('Paused'));
audio.addEventListener('ended', () => console.log('Finished'));
audio.addEventListener('timeupdate', () => {
  const percent = (audio.currentTime / audio.duration) * 100;
  console.log('Progress:', percent + '%');
});

audio.addEventListener('loadedmetadata', () => {
  console.log('Duration:', audio.duration);
});

audio.addEventListener('error', (e) => {
  console.log('Error:', e.target.error.code);
});

Audio Format Support

Format Compatibility

Format MIME Type Browser Support
MP3 audio/mpeg All modern browsers
WAV audio/wav Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge
Ogg Vorbis audio/ogg Chrome, Firefox, Opera
FLAC audio/flac Chrome, Firefox, Edge

Features

Built-in Controls

Play/pause, progress bar, volume controls included.

Compact UI

Minimal player interface, responsive design.

Multiple Formats

Fallback to different formats for compatibility.

Playback Rate Control

Speed up or slow down playback (1x, 1.25x, 1.5x, 2x).

Looping

Automatically repeat on completion.

Time Management

Seek to any point, show current time and duration.

Use Cases

Podcasts

Host and play podcast episodes.

Music

Stream or serve music tracks.

Audio Books

Chapter-based audio content.

Sound Effects

Interactive audio for games or apps.

Voice Notes

User-generated audio recordings.

Language Learning

Pronunciation guides and lessons.

Accessibility

Responsive Behavior

This component automatically adapts to different screen sizes:

Mobile (< 768px)

On mobile devices:

  • Optimized for small screens
  • Touch-friendly spacing maintained (44px minimum)
  • Text wraps naturally without overflow
  • Content density optimized for readability

Desktop (1024px+)

On desktop displays:

  • Enhanced visual features
  • Hover states and transitions
  • Full keyboard navigation support
  • Optimal spacing and sizing

Framework Integration

Responsive behavior is automatic:

  • Framework handles media queries
  • No custom CSS required
  • Mobile-first approach applied
  • Accessibility maintained at all sizes

Best Practices

  • Always use the <audio> element semantically
  • Provide transcripts for spoken content
  • Use controls attribute for player UI
  • Test keyboard navigation (space, arrows)
  • Provide fallback content for unsupported browsers
  • Label audio content clearly before the player
  • Consider autoplay policies (may require muted)

AXIOM01 Philosophy

Why Use Native <audio>

The native <audio> element embodies AXIOM01 principles:

  • Semantic: <audio> tells the browser exactly what it is
  • Accessible: Built-in controls work with keyboard and screen readers
  • Minimal Code: No custom JavaScript needed for basic playback
  • Progressive Enhancement: Works without JavaScript
  • Browser Native: Uses OS-level audio codecs
  • Performance: Lightweight and efficient

Custom audio players require hundreds of lines of code and often break accessibility. Use <audio> first.

[Ax01]
